# Atomic Learning: a plain-English edition of a real textbook

> We don't ask what kind of learner you are. We give every learner several grounded routes to the same idea. We also show you the receipts.

## Concepts in prerequisite order

- [Vectors](llms-full.txt#vectors): A vector is a fixed-length array whose elements are scalar values.
- [Dot Product](llms-full.txt#dot-product): The dot product sums products of corresponding elements from two vectors.
- [Matrix–Vector Product](llms-full.txt#matrix-vector-product): A matrix–vector product produces a vector whose entries are dot products between matrix rows and the input vector.
- [Softmax](llms-full.txt#softmax): Softmax transforms neural network outputs into a valid discrete probability distribution.
- [Queries, Keys, and Values](llms-full.txt#qkv): An attention mechanism uses query–key compatibility to determine weights applied to values.
- [Attention Pooling](llms-full.txt#attention-pooling): Attention pooling forms a linear combination of stored values.
- [Self-Attention](llms-full.txt#self-attention): Self-attention lets every token attend to every other token when constructing token representations.
- [Positional Encoding](llms-full.txt#positional-encoding): Positional encodings provide token-order information to a model as additional token-associated inputs.
- [Softmax Preserves Ordering](llms-full.txt#softmax-ordering): Softmax preserves the ordering of its inputs, so the largest input also identifies the highest-probability class.
- [Vector Norm](llms-full.txt#vector-norm): A vector norm measures the magnitude or size of a vector.
